mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value < 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Triggered instances yield positive 5-day returns in over 65% of cases, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below -0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real personal income and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects robust corporate output capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative mean 20-day returns during late-cycle periods, or predictive accuracy drops below 50% in regime-filtered samples.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms labor market resilience, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS revisions correlate with negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation acceleration above the 0.4 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL threshold breaches are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and stable real personal income indicates corporate profit margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns (Rank IC near 0) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals supply chain bottlenecks and inventory depletion, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recovery phases yield positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances, or predictive accuracy fails to exceed random chance.
